title: Delayed Opening March 3, 2026
description: Due to conditions related to the winter storm the park will have a delayed opening on Tuesday, March 3. Park facilities, including the visitor center will now open at 11 a.m. Portions of Park Central Road may remain closed.

title: Area Closure for National Wildfire
description: Big Cypress National Preserve has issued a temporary area closure in order to ensure firefighter and public safety during wildfire activity. For more information and to view specific area restriction map, please click the link.

title: Skyline Drive Status
description: Skyline Drive from Thornton Gap Entrance Station to Swift Run Gap Entrance Station (miles 31.5 to 65.5) is now open. All other sections remain closed due to hazardous winter road conditions.

title: The Virgin River Narrows and all routes through the Narrows are closed
description: The Narrows is closed due to increased water flow from a snow melt event.

title: Greenbush Segment — Caution: Logging Activities
description: A timber harvest is taking place on the Greenbush Segment. The work affects about 0.1 mile of Trail between Shelter #5 and the Greenbush Picnic Area. Heavy machinery may be in the area. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Annual and lifetime passes not available for purchase
description: Annual and lifetime passes are not currently available for purchase at Puʻuhonua o Hōnaunau National Historical Park. As an alternative, you can purchase an annual pass online.

title: Beware of Areas of Quicksand
description: Quicksand may be present near the shoreline and in drainages throughout the park. It can appear dry and firm on the surface but may suddenly give way. Recognize unstable, shifting, or unusually soft ground, and use caution when entering through these areas.

title: Quicksand Present at Lakeshore Edge of the Rainbow Bridge Trail
description: Quicksand may be present near the shoreline and in drainages throughout the park. It can appear dry and firm on the surface but may suddenly give way. Recognize unstable, shifting, or unusually soft ground, and use caution when entering through these areas.

title: Blasting near Memorial
description: Blasting activity is occurring in the Coronado National Forest, Huachuca Mountains. Visitors may hear loud noise and notice dust in the air, but the impacted area is outside Coronado National Memorial.

title: Paradise Road Status: Gate closed at Longmire, check tomorrow for status.
description: 03/03/2026 The road is open to Longmire. All vehicles, including 4WD and AWD, must carry tire chains and be ready to use them. The gate at Longmire to Paradise is closed for the night to support public safety. Check in the morning for updated status of the gate at Longmire, Wednesday 03/04/2026.

title: Fencing replacement at Battery Spencer through March 2026
description: Park staff, in collaboration with the Golden Gate Bridge District, will be performing trail maintenance and fencing replacement at the popular Battery Spencer in the Marin Headlands. Please pay attention to safety signage and respect any temporary closures.

title: Planned Power Outage 3/10/26
description: A planned power outage will occur starting 8:00 a.m. on Tuesday March 10th, likely lasting until 4:00 p.m. in Furnace Creek and Stovepipe Wells. Some amenities may be unavailable such as internet and gas. Please plan accordingly.

title: Road to Rim Village is Temporarily CLOSED
description: The road from Park Headquarters to Rim Village is CLOSED due to the incoming storm. No view of the lake is accessible by vehicle at this time. Please use caution if driving in the park. All Oregon Chain Laws apply.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Road from HQ to Rim Village Remains CLOSED.

title: Invasive Vegetation Removal
description: Watch for crews treating invasive plants along Snake Bight Trail, Rowdy Bend Road, and other Flamingo areas. Approved herbicides are being used. Stay alert for workers, equipment, and posted signs.

title: Elephant Hill Access Rd and Cave Spring Rd to close temporarily March 26
description: The unpaved Elephant Hill Access Road and Cave Spring Road, and associated parking lots, will close temporarily for necessary road maintenance on March 26, 2026. The roads and parking lots will be closed to all uses until the work is complete, which should be later that same day.

title: High Levels of Visitation
description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center.

title: High Levels of Visitation
description: The park will be experiencing high levels of visitation from March through May. Expect limited parking, long lines, and crowded areas. Please have a credit or debit card ready or valid park pass with ID when checking in at the visitor center. Parking or stopping is not permitted on the roads outside the park gate at any time.

title: Tsankawi Unit of Bandelier Temporarily Closed
description: Beginning February 25th, 2026, Bandelier National Monument's Tsankawi Unit will temporarily be closed for a Trails and Visitor Improvement Project. A helicopter will be onsite to move materials from the parking lot to the top of the mesa. During these operations, Tsankawi will be unsafe for visitors. The main Frijoles Canyon section of Bandelier including the Visitor Center, Main Pueblo Loop Trail, Park Store, and Cafe will remain open.

title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
description: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400 or visit https://www.dcwater.com/about-dc-water/media/potomac-interceptor-collapse.

title: Hurricane Ridge Road Status, 3/2 - 3/5
description: Hurricane Ridge Road remains closed Monday through Thursday during the winter season. The road is scheduled to reopen Friday, March 6th, at 9am, weather and road conditions permitting.

title: K-177 Highway Closed from Council Grove South to the Preserve, Use Detour
description: Beginning Monday, March 2, K-177 will close for a bridge replacement from Council Grove to the U.S. 50/K-177 Strong City intersection. Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve will remain open but must be accessed from the south or follow the detour west out of Council Grove. K-177 thru traffic will be directed to follow the state detour on U.S. 56, U.S. 56/U.S. 77 and K-150. KDOT uses state highways for official detour routes for safety, weight and maintenance concerns. Go to KDOT website for detour map.

title: Playground in Pine Grove Picnic Area Closed
description: The park is improving accessibility at the playground in Pine Grove Picnic Area. During construction, until approximately the end of March 2026, the playground is closed and construction vehicles may be in the area. Use caution when hiking in the area and follow all posted closure notices.

title: No dock access to Rainbow Bridge
description: At current lake levels, Rainbow Bridge docks do not reach the land. Dock-to-trail access to Rainbow Bridge will not be available until water levels rise again. Restrooms at the dock will remain open. This alert will stay up until the situation changes. Check the link for current lake information.

title: Critical Backcountry Updates/Closures
description: Visit this link for the inner canyon weather forecast, current trail closures, today's heat risk, and drinking water availability.

title: Belmont-Paul Women's Equality NM currently closed
description: Belmont-Paul Women's Equality National Monument is currently closed due to ongoing repairs. We apologize for any inconvenience.

title: Visitor Center Restrooms Closed for Renovation
description: The restrooms at the Kris Eggle Visitor Center will be closed from the end of February until April for renovation. Temporary restrooms will be available in the visitor center parking lot. Visitors in need of more permanent facilities are advised to use those at the Twin Peaks Campground.

title: Temporary Museum Closure
description: The park museum in the visitor center is temporarily closed for the installation of new exhibits. All other areas within the visitor center such as the welcome desk, theater, and bookstore will remain open during the project. The museum will reopen in the Spring of 2026.

title: Road Open To: Mile 13 (Mountain Vista)
description: The Denali Park Road is open to Mile 13, Mountain Vista. Wintry conditions beyond that point prevent vehicle travel, though pedestrian travel is permitted. Be prepared for winter driving conditions to reach Mile 13.

title: Visitor Center Hours of Operation
description: The Visitor Center is open Friday - Sunday from 9:00am to 4:00pm

title: OSV Status
description: 2/28/26 MD OSV to KM 21.8, VA OSV Open to the Hook

title: Trail update 2/28/26
description: Most trails are open and ready for the public. Those that remain closed are the Charleston Road/ Goudey Trail past the Cherokee Path and the portion of Cherokee Path between the stockade fort and the Charleston Road.

title: Great Hall, Faneuil Hall Closures: March 2026
description: Due to scheduled events, the Great Hall will be closed on March 14, 19, 20, 21, and 27. The Visitor Center Desk on the market floor will remain open.

title: No Potable Water March 2-10
description: Due to a construction project in the park, water will be turned off to all facilities from Monday, March 2nd to approximately Tuesday, March 10th. Portable restroom facilities will be available for use in the park during this timeframe.

title: Several Trails Close For Peregrine Falcon Nesting Season
description: Effective March 1, areas including Jordan Cliffs Trail, Penobscot East Trail, Precipice Trail, and Valley Cove Trail are closed to protect nesting peregrine falcons. The parking lot for the Precipice Trail is also closed. Rangers strictly enforce closures.

title: Loop Road Closure
description: The majority of the Loop Road is closed to vehicles. However, a 1.5 mile segment to vehicles is open from the Visitor's Center to Devils Orchard. The remainder of the road will open in spring 2026, weather permitting. Past Devils Orchard, the Loop Road is open to walking, hiking, wheelchairs, cross-country skiing, and snowshoeing as weather permits. Please plan accordingly.

title: Ocean Beach Fire Season in Effect March 9 through October 31, 2025
description: Fires allowed ONLY in park provided concrete fire rings between Stairwells 15 and 20. Park will install fire rings on March 9. Do NOT make fire rings using rocks, logs or other items. For more information, visit the beach fire regulations link below.

title: Shark Valley Entrance Delays
description: Expect long wait times to enter the Shark Valley area particularly between 10am-3pm. The Shark Valley parking lot can fill up quickly and becomes 'one car out, one car in'. We recommend arriving 1.5-2 hours early for any scheduled tram tours. Please call the visitor center at 305-221-8776 with any questions about access

title: Inclement Weather Closure
description: Closure due to hazardous road conditions due to weather conditions. Recreation is authorized in sections closed for winter weather only.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Inclement Weather Closure

title: Lehman Caves Closure
description: Lehman Caves and the Lehman Caves Visitor Center are closed to the public as work begins on a six-month project to replace the cave’s 48-year-old, failing electric lights. Project completion is planned for summer 2026. The rest of Great Basin National Park remains open.

title: Water Interruption at Wildcat Campground and Glen Campground
description: Water is currently unavailable at Wildcat and Glen Campgrounds. Visitors should plan to bring an adequate supply of water for their trip. Refunds are available through Recreation.gov for those who wish to cancel their reservations.

title: Park Auditorium Closed Until Further Notice
description: Due to some damage from recent storms, the auditorium will be closed until further notice. We are sorry for the inconvenience.

title: Headquarters Visitor Center Closed 2/17 through the week of 3/8
description: Headquarters Visitor Center will be temporarily closed for a building improvement project from February 17th through the week of March 8. The parking lot and restrooms at this location will remain open.

title: Navajo Loop Trail Closed
description: Mudslides in multiple areas of the Two Bridges Trail have prompted a safety closure. The Wall Street Trail remains in its normal winter closure. As a result, the entire Navajo Loop Trail is closed until further notice.

title: The Painted Desert Oasis Gift Shop, Gas Station and Restaurant are Open!
description: The Painted Desert Oasis gift shop, gas station and restaurant located at the north entrance of the park are open, and restrooms are available during construction. Visitor center operations are currently located at the Painted Desert Inn, two miles past the entrance station.

title: Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center Closed for Renovations
description: Starting November 20, 2025 the Fredericksburg Battlefield Visitor Center will be closed while updates are made to building systems. Front Desk operations including visitor orientation will move to the adjacent Fredericksburg Battlefield Bookstore. The Chancellorsville Visitor Center remains open to visitors 9am - 5pm all week.

title: Restrooms and Water Fountains Unavailable at Powers Island and Akers Mill
description: Restrooms and water fountains are closed at the Powers Island unit until repairs are complete. Powers Island visitors may use facilities across the river at Cochran Shoals - Interstate North. Restrooms and water fountain at Sandy Point (Akers Mill, West Palisades) are closed for the winter.

title: Unpaved Salt Valley Rd & Willow Spring Rd to close temporarily March 23-24
description: Maintenance crews will apply road surface treatments to the unpaved Salt Valley Road and Willow Spring Road March 23-24, 2026. The roads will close to all uses while work is occurring but should reopen later the same day.

title: All Trails on Shiloh Battlefield Closed
description: Due to tree and limb hazards from the recent ice storm, all trails on the battlefield are considered closed. Cleanup operations have begun on the park. Please be aware of workers and equipment on the roadways.

title: Icy Conditions Persist After Recent Storm
description: The parking lot is open but snow and icy conditions persist. If you decide to visit, please use extreme caution.

title: Temporary Closure of the Lower Wilderness – Prescribed Burn
description: To ensure public safety, a large portion of the Lower Buffalo Wilderness will be closed to horse and foot traffic between Feb. 22 and March 23, while the National Park Service conducts a prescribed burn within Buffalo National River. The river corridor will remain open, with canoes and john boats allowed, and camping permitted on gravel bars.

title: Muddy roads
description: Four wheel drive is recommended on Black Canyon road due to mud. Backcountry roads may also be muddy and more difficult to navigate than usual.

title: Temporary Road Closures Possible Due to Wildfire Activity
description: SR 29 and I-75 may be closed periodically due to smoke impacts from the National Wildfire. Please be patient and use caution when in the area.

title: Playalinda District will return to normal operations starting 2/26/26
description: With the rollback of the Artemis II rocket, Canaveral National Seashore will return to normal operations (6am to 6pm) in the Playalinda District starting Thursday February 26th 2026. We will continue to support the Artemis II mission as they plan to roll back out in April.

title: March 2: Fishing, ORV, Boat Ramp, Archery Passes on Sale at Recreation.gov
description: On March 2, 2026, at 10am, the following passes will be available for purchase ONLINE ONLY through Recreation.gov: Fishing Access (Parkwide), Off-Road Vehicle (Breezy Point), Boat Ramp Parking (Great Kills Park) and Archery Parking (Floyd Bennett Field). Sale Period March 2-March 31. All vehicle decals MUST be picked up by April 12.

title: Restrooms Out of Service
description: The restrooms at Fort Washington Park are temporarily out of order. Portable toilets are currently available for visitor use.

title: Parking impacted due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project
description: The Lower Farm parking lot near the Mule Barn is closed due to the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. The Upper parking lot located near the Orangery has reduced capacity due to a partial closure because of the Legacy Restoration Fund Project. To learn more about parking at Hampton NHS, click the link or visit the alerts page and then scroll down to the area that talks about parking.

title: Glenmont Mansion and Grounds Closed
description: Glenmont, the Edison family home and surrounding estate grounds, are currently closed until further notice due to operational and safety considerations. The Laboratory Complex remains open Thursday–Sunday, 10 a.m.–4 p.m. Please check this page for updates.

title: Park Headquarters Phones are Down
description: Phones at park headquarters are currently down. Please call the visitor center at Painted Desert Inn 928-235-2467 or the Visitor Services office 928-235-2391 between 8 am and 5 pm. You can also send us an email at pefo_information@nps.gov.

title: Hazardous Conditions exist in caves
description: Hazardous conditions exist in caves due to high water rain events. When encountering flowing water anywhere in the park use extreme caution. Conditions can change rapidly. A mere 6 inches of fast-moving flood water can knock over an adult. Turn Around Don't Drown®. The park will update this alert when conditions change.

title: Illinois Memorial Closed for Restoration
description: The memorial itself closed for restoration starting on September 16, 2025. Starting on March 2, 2026, access to Tour Stops 2 and 3 will be temporarily closed. The closure point will be at the Shirley House. Visitors will still be able to continue north along Union Avenue and access the remainder of the Tour Road. This section will remain closed for the duration of the Illinois Memorial Restoration. The project is expected to be completed by September 2026.

title: Tioga Road (Hwy 120 through the park) and Glacier Point Road are closed
description: Tioga Road (continuation of Highway 120 through the park), Glacier Point Road beyond Badger Pass, and Mariposa Grove Road are closed for the season due to snow. Snow is possible on other roads; be prepared for tire chain requirements and other road closures. Call 209/372-0200 (then 1, 1) to check road conditions.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Tioga and Glacier Point Roads winter closure

title: Verona Segment — Badger Prairie Park Construction
description: Construction at Badger Prairie Park may mean fencing and a small Trail detour west of the parking area. Restrooms and shelters may be closed. The Dispersed Camping Area is available but may have no restroom or water availability. The Trail is open. Stay alert and follow posted detour signs.

title: Indian Creek Segment — Caution: Machinery and Parking Closure
description: Recent logging near CTH-E (15th Street) may mean heavy machinery and log piles in the area. The parking lot on CTH-E remains closed to hikers. The Ice Age Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Hemlock Creek Segment — Caution: Logging Activities
description: Logging operations along the Hemlock Creek Segment may mean heavy machinery and trucks in the area. The Ice Age Trail follows a logging road used for storage and transport of logs. The Trail is open. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Monticello Segment — Stewart Tunnel Closed
description: The Stewart Tunnel is closed indefinitely pending reconstruction. The Trail is closed at both intersections of Tunnel Road. Use Tunnel Road to detour around the closure. Check with the DNR for details: dnr.wisconsin.gov/topic/parks/badger/tunnel.

title: St. Croix Falls Segment — Construction & Intermittent Closures
description: Construction activity near the St. Croix Falls school complex may mean machinery in the area. The Ice Age Trail remains open. Trail closures and detours are anticipated in the spring. To bypass when hiking eastbound, head north on Jackson Street, east on Louisiana Street, and south on Blanding Woods Road.

title: Brooklyn Wildlife Segment — Timber Stand Improvement Project
description: A timber stand improvement project is underway in the Brooklyn Wildlife Area north of Hughes Road and will continue through the end of March. The Ice Age Trail remains open but expect heavy machinery in the area. Stay alert. Remain on the trail. Use caution. Keep dogs leashed.

title: Janesville Segment — Partial Trail Closure
description: Due to sewer rehabilitation, the Ice Age Trail along Palmer Drive is closed from Sharon Road to East Racine Street. The closure is expected to remain in effect through the end of March. Please follow the posted detour.

title: Point Beach Segment — Partial Trail Closure March 4 to June 15
description: The Ice Age Trail and parking area on the west side of CTH-O in Point Beach State Forest will be closed March 4 through June 15 during a timber harvest for public safety. All trails and parking areas west of CTH-O will be closed. To bypass when hiking eastbound, take CTH-O north and continue west around the bend where the road becomes CTH-V. The 2.1-mile reroute replaces 2.0 miles of Trail.

title: Water Conservation Measures begins Feb. 23, 2026
description: Grand Canyon National Park is implementing water conservation measures on the South Rim following a power outage that impacted the SCADA system at the Havasupai Gardens pumphouse. As a result, water is currently unable to be pumped to the South Rim.

title: No Horse-Riding or Pack Animal Permits Issued Until Further Notice
description: Horse-riding and pack animal permits will not be issued until further notice. Horses and pack animals should not be brought into the park until permits resume.

title: Condition of Turkey Creek Trail
description: The South Turkey Creek Trail, located near the Harbor Bay Campground Area, is currently open for hiking for the first mile; however, the remainder of the trail is underwater and cannot be safely accessed.

title: Heavy Traffic and High Visitation Alert
description: Pinnacles experiences extremely high visitation on weekends, holidays, and during favorable weather, especially in the spring. Expect long lines and delays between 10:00 am and 3:00 pm. If you're planning to hike the High Peaks, Condor Gulch, Balconies, or Moses Spring Trail, it’s recommended to arrive before 8:00 am (parking may fill earlier). Additionally, campground access maybe temporarily delayed during peak periods of visitation when ALL incoming traffic is held.

title: Willow Beach Road Repaving Is Complete
description: The Willow Beach Road has now been fully repaved and striped with no gravel sections. Drivers use caution as crews are finishing up grading the road shoulders during the week.

title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specifica details on the closure.

title: Temporary Closure of Right Lane of Clara Barton Parkway
description: Temporary Closure of the Clara Barton Parkway Right Lane at Site 17, wooded areas between C&O Canal and Lock 10 parking lot, north to Lock 14, required to address ongoing safety concerns from DC Water emergency sanitary sewer overflow & bypass and pipe repair operations. Click on "More" below for specifica details on the closure.

title: Upper Cliff Dwelling Tours Full
description: Upper Cliff Dwelling tours are fully booked for the current season, February 7th to May 3rd. Cancellations may occur, but they are rare. Reservations for next season will be taken starting October 1, 2026, for November 2026-April 2027 season.

title: Barataria Visitor Center and Trails are Closed
description: Because of upcoming demolition and construction of the trails and Visitor Center, all trails at the Barataria Preserve and the Visitor Center are closed, with the exception of Twin Canals Trail. Please join us Wednesdays and Thursdays 10:00 am - 2:00 pm at the Lafitte's Barataria Museum and Wetland Trace for weekly programming, and guided hikes along the Wetland Trace trail at 1:00 pm Wednesday and Thursday.

title: Winter Hours for Sheriff's House Welcome Center
description: The park will be implementing winter hours for the Sheriff's House in New Castle, DE. Winter hours means that the Sheriff's House will be closed to the public from the first Sunday of January (01/04/26) to the first Thursday in March (03/05/26).

title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
description: Sunset Crater Volcano National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions. The monument will open at 11am.

title: 2-hour delayed opening 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather.
description: Walnut Canyon National Monument will have a 2-hour delayed opening on 20, February 2026 due to inclement weather and area icy road conditions . The monument will open at 11am.

title: NPS 300 Road Conditions
description: NPS 300 to Big Tubes contains deep, muddy ruts and has not been evaluated since the most recent snowfall. A high clearance, four-wheel-drive vehicle is required to attempt this road. Conditions will deteriorate as snow melts.

title: Road workers on Park Road in Davis Bayou
description: Phase II of the Davis Bayou Bicycle and Pedestrian Path is in process. Visitors may encounter minor traffic delays and flaggers on Eagle Point and Park Road.

title: Opal Beach Pavilion Restroom Operating Hours
description: The restrooms at Opal Beach Complex will have scheduled temporary closures until further notice. The new temporary operating schedule will be Monday through Thursday, only pavilion B1 and D restrooms will be open. On Friday, Saturday, and Sunday, only pavilions A, B2 and E will be open. Pavilion C is permanently closed for repairs.

title: Congress Hall Temporary Closure
description: Congress Hall will close to visitors on Monday, February 23 through Friday, April 24, 2026. Contractors will replace fire alarm and security systems to ensure that our visitors, structures and artifacts are protected by up-to-date equipment. These improvements are one more way that the National Park Service is preparing Independence National Historical Park for the upcoming 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

title: Cave Tours Closed For Winter
description: Cave tours are closed for the winter season. Tours are expected to resume March 21, 2026. Subject to change.

title: Sidewalk scaffolding at Federal Hall
description: Protective sidewalk scaffolding has been erected around the building to protect the public from falling materials. Later this year, additional scaffolding will be installed while we conduct an in-depth condition assessment of the building façade.

title: Trail closures possible with winter weather
description: Portions of trails may close when snow or ice are present. Please check in at the visitor center for current trail conditions.

title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
description: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400.

title: DC Water Sewer Overflow
description: DC Water is managing a sanitary sewer overflow from the Potomac Interceptor near Lock 10. Anyone with questions or in need of support can reach out to DC Water’s 24-Hour Command Center at (202) 612-3400.

title: Ice caves are CLOSED due to unstable ice conditions.
description: The ice shelf near Meyers Beach at Apostle Islands National Lakeshore was broken up during a strong winter storm that impacted the region beginning on 2/17. Access to the ice caves within the National Lakeshore is closed to visitation.

title: Trip-planning tips for 2026
description: Planning to visit Arches this year? Stay tuned on this website for updates on road and trail conditions and how to minimize waits and crowds while maximizing trail time and fun.

title: State Route 20 Closed MP134
description: State Route 20 is closed at milepost 134 (Ross Dam Trailhead) for the winter. For more information click the link to the WSDOT real time map.

title: Telephone System is Temporarily Out of Service
description: The phone system at Sagamore Hill is temporarily out of service. Please contact the park in the interim via email at sahi_information@nps.gov.

title: Drakes Beach Road open 10 am to 5 pm to better protect elephant seal pups.
description: Drakes Beach Road now only open 10 am to 5 pm to better protect elephant seal pups. Northern elephant seals have returned to Drakes Beach and given birth close to beach access points. To better protect the pups during this important time, the parking lot will only be open when park staff and volunteers are present, every day from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm. The beach itself will remain closed, but seals are viewable from the parking lot and Ken Patrick Visitor Center area during these ho

title: Overlook Park Lot Unavailable After Dark
description: The park parking lot at 72 McBride Extension is open from dawn to dusk every day. Any vehicles left in the parking lot after dusk (i.e. sunset) are subject to towing at owner's expense.

title: Historic Homes Closed for Season
description: The historic homes are closed for the winter season and are expected to open May 1. Park grounds are open daily sunrise to sunset. The Visitor Center is open 11:00 am to 4:00 pm Mondays through Fridays. The Visitor Center is closed Saturdays and Sundays. The Visitor Center is closed on wintertime federal holidays, except for Presidents Day.

title: Speed Limits are Much Lower Inside Park than Surrounding Highways
description: Speed limits for the park include 30 MPH near the park boundary and Great Sand Dunes Sign, and as low as 10 MPH near the Headquarters and Entrance Station. Speed limits are in place to protect park visitors, pedestrians, and wildlife in the park. Please drive carefully and responsibly.

title: Winter Closures
description: The following areas are closed for the winter season: [1] South Rim Road beyond the visitor center. [2] All North Rim facilities and vehicle access. North Rim Road is closed to vehicles and gated at the park boundary. [3] East Portal Road--the road and its closure are managed by the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation. The NPS-managed East Portal Campground is also closed for winter. Pedestrians and bicycles are currently allowed on closed roads, conditions permitting. Use caution.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Winter Closures

title: Long-term Closures Due to Wildfires
description: Due to the effects of the 2025 South Rim Fire, South Rim Campground and Rim Rock Trail from South Rim Campground to Tomichi Point are closed until further notice.

title: Restrooms Intermittently Closed at Montezuma Castle for Plumbing Repairs
description: Due to extensive plumbing repairs, the restrooms at Montezuma Castle will be closed intermittently for repairs at various times. When closed, portable toilets are available on site. Please plan accordingly.

title: Tire Chain Restrictions in Effect - Chains Required to Visit Giant Sequoias
description: Chain restrictions are in effect on some park roads. All vehicles must carry chains or cables in some areas, including 4WD and AWD vehicles. Install chains when required. For current road information, call (559) 565-3341, (press 1, then 1)

title: Intermittent phone issues
description: Posted Feb. 15, 2026: Valley Forge's phone system is experiencing intermittent outages. If you have questions about Washington's Birthday celebration on 2/16, please check out the event listing at https://www.nps.gov/planyourvisit/event-details.htm?id=6BB581B8-98C5-3D38-0AF3A032735016A5.

title: Park Bookstore Has Gone Cashless
description: We are excited to announce that our park store, operated by Western National Parks, is now operating on a cashless basis. For your convenience, they accept all major credit and debit cards, as well as mobile payment options.

title: Deer management operations
description: From January 2 to April 13, 2026, the park will be conducting deer management operations outside of park operating hours. Per the Harpers Ferry National Historical Park Superintendent’s Compendium, trails close 30 minutes after sunset.

title: Buildings located on the farm side are closed
description: Buildings located on the farm side of Hampton National Historic Site are closed due to construction related to the ongoing Legacy Restoration Fund Project. Visit the project webpage for more information or click the link to learn more.

title: Off Season Tours by Appointment Unavailable on Dates from February - May
description: Off season tours by appointment are not available on the following dates in February, March, April, and May: February 13; February 20; March 9 - April 3; April 6 - 10; May 15 - 27.

title: Passenger Ferry Dock Closed
description: The ferry dock servicing the Cape Lookout Lighthouse area is closed for repairs. Visitors will have to use an alternative route on the beach to access the boardwalk. Visitors should wear sturdy, closed toe shoes because of potential hazards.

title: Angels Landing Pilot Permit Program
description: Everyone who hikes Angels Landing needs to have a permit. Learn why you need a permit and how to get one. This requirement does not affect entering the park or riding the park shuttle (when it is running).

title: Haleakalā Visitor Center Closed 2/19 through 4/10
description: Haleakalā Visitor Center will be temporarily closed for a building improvement project from February 19th through April 10th. Sunrise operations will continue as normal and restrooms will remain open.

title: RV and trailer precautions: be aware of vehicle restrictions before visit.
description: As the busy season ramps up, traffic will be denser and can result in delays and complications particularly for owners of longer vehicles. The Tucson Mountain District (west side) of the park can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in total length on the loop drive and has RV parking available at the Visitor Center, and the Rincon Mountain District (east side) can accommodate vehicles up to 40ft in length on the loop drive but has no RV parking available at the Visitor Center.

title: No Water at Hosmer Campground
description: As of September 30, 2025, water at Hosmer Campground in the Summit District is shutoff due to a waterline break. Water is available 24-hours at Headquarters Visitor Center one mile up the road. At this point we don't have an estimate for when repairs will be complete.

title: Phone Outage
description: Our site is experiencing connectivity issues with our phone system, and we are unable to receive calls. Please call 9-1-1 for emergencies. For all other inquiries, please email biho_visitor_information@nps.gov for a quicker response. Thank you for your patience while we resolve this issue.

title: Road to the Trailhead Parking Lot is Closed to Vehicles for the Season
description: The road leading to the trailhead parking lot at the Big Hole National Battlefield is closed to vehicles for the season but remains open for use by snowshoers & cross-country skiers. The road to the visitor center remains open to vehicles year-round.

title: 12/12/2025 Update - Parkway and Campground closed from MP 181-204.
description: The parkway is closed to all traffic from MP 181-204. The section of the parkway, including the Jeff Busby Campground and day use area, has been closed since June 9, 2025, to allow for the removal and replacement of failed pavement and culverts. While the project was initially expected to be completed by the end of summer, unforeseen site conditions and weather delays have extended the timeline. A definitive reopening date is not available at this time. Construction will continue into 2026.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Road Repavement

title: State Route 148 Closed Through Cedar Breaks National Monument
description: State Route 148 through Cedar Breaks National Monument is now closed for its routine winter season. Plan alternate routes and check back for updates. Visit the Utah Department of Transportation website for more route information: www.udottraffic.utah.gov

title: Application for Special Use Permit
description: Each applicant is required to complete a new "Application For Special Use Permit" prior to any new permit being issued

title: Roadwork Along Lakeshore Drive at the Alfred Smith Water Treatment Plant
description: Starting February 9th, 2026, roadwork will begin on the east shoulder of Lakeshore Drive between the water treatment plant and the Lake Mead Fish Hatchery for the installation of a pipeline. Flaggers will be present during the project, but only minimal traffic delays are anticipated at times that traffic is restricted to one lane. The project is expected to be completed in a few months.

title: Folger Park Closed for Rehabilitation
description: Folger Park will remain closed until April 30, 2026, at 11:59 pm. This temporary closure is necessary to provide for visitor safety during a comprehensive rehabilitation project to restore park resources and improve the visitor experience.

title: Prescribed burning may occur in the park between February 14 - March 15
description: Wilson’s Creek National Battlefield plans to conduct a prescribed burn in the park some time between February 14, 2026 – March 15, 2026 dependent on weather. For additional information on whether prescribed fire operations and closures will occur on a given day, call the park’s Visitor Center at 417-732-2662.

title: Old Post Office Tower is Closed
description: The Old Post Office Tower has been closed to the public while the building’s owner, the General Services Administration, determines the future ownership and management of the Old Post Office building, including tower operations and public access. The National Park Service is proud to have operated the tower for the past 42 years, welcoming millions of visitors during that time.

title: Old Post Office Tower is Closed
description: The Old Post Office Tower has been closed to the public while the building’s owner, the General Services Administration, determines the future ownership and management of the Old Post Office building, including tower operations and public access. The National Park Service is proud to have operated the tower for the past 42 years, welcoming millions of visitors during that time.

title: South Shore Quinault Road Washed Out Beyond Park Boundary
description: South Shore Road – which is maintained by Jefferson County – is closed due to a major wash-out. For more information, visit the Jefferson County website.

title: Temporary Visitor Contact Station at Andrew Johnson NHS.
description: The Andrew Johnson NHS Visitor Center is currently closed for renovations. A temporary visitor contact station is located inside the Memorial Building (housing Johnson's original Tailor Shop), which is adjacent to the visitor center. Renovation work is expected to take approximately 5 months.

title: Annual Closure to Protect Nesting Peregrine Falcons
description: To provide peregrine falcons adequate space for nesting, portions of Maryland Heights will be closed from Feb 15 to July 31. This includes several rock outcroppings near the overlook and certain climbing walls. The Maryland Heights Trail is OPEN.

title: Staircase, Adjacent Wilderness Areas Closed due to Bear Gulch Fire Impacts
description: For a full list of closed trails and campsites, visit our Fire Conditions and Updates page. For archived fire content, including news releases and maps, visit the Bear Gulch Fire InciWeb page.

title: Access to Bluestone NSR through Bluestone State Park is closed
description: Due to road damage, the road to access Bluestone National Scenic River through Bluestone State Park is closed. The road will remain completely closed as repairs are made. It is expected to reopen in late April or early May.

title: Trail Closure at Scott’s Run Nature Preserve (McLean, Virginia)
description: Scott’s Run Nature Preserve in McLean, Virginia will close, beginning February 16th, for an urgent sewer project to renew and rehabilitate the Scott's Run Siphon wastewater facility. All trails and parking lots in the park are closed for approximately three months.

title: Vehicle Weight Limit at Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens Entrance
description: Due to structural conditions at the driveway entrance, access to the Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens main entrance and parking lot is limited to vehicles with a gross vehicle weight rating (GVWR) of 16,000 pounds or less.

title: Replace Bocce Ball Court Retaining Wall
description: Project work begins on Monday, February 9 to replace the bocce ball 50-foot retaining wall in order to improve existing infrastructure and enhance visitor safety. Several parking spots on lower Van Ness will be fenced off and the bocce ball court will not be accessible for the duration of the project, slated through August 2026. Please be aware of machinery, work, and signs.

title: Icy Conditions Remain Especially on Trails
description: Visitor Centers at the Eastern Front, Five Forks and City Point remain open as normally scheduled but icy conditions persist. We advise caution, especially on trails, in parking lots and shaded areas.

title: West and East Border Roads closed for public safety
description: South Puerto Blanco Drive remains open. All border roads remain closed (the dirt roads directly along the border and all crossover roads leading from South Puerto Blanco Drive to the border) to all public vehicle and pedestrian traffic for public safety.

title: Phone Lines Currently Down
description: The Whitman Mission NHS phone lines are currently down. If you need to contact us, please email whmi_interpretation. For field trip scheduling, please email whmi_education@nps.gov

title: Construction at Muir Home Site
description: Construction is ongoing at the home site and the surrounding areas. Disruption to normal visitation may occur.

title: No potable water at Chaco
description: There is currently no potable water at the Chaco visitor center or Gallo campground. We do not have an estimate on when potable water will be restored. If you are visiting the park, please bring any water you need with you for your visit. Porta potties are available at the visitor center and campground. If you have any questions, please feel free to call the visitor center anytime between 9-5: 505-786-7014.

title: Temporary closure of portions of the Carter Barron parking lot
description: The south and southwest sections of the parking lot at the Carter Barron Amphitheater (US Reservation 339) are temporarily closed to allow the safe operation of District of Columbia snow storage activities as a result of Winter Storm Fern. See the Record of Determination for more information.

title: Life Jackets Required!
description: By Nebraska State law, all children 13 years old and younger must WEAR a USCG approved life jacket to float the Niobrara NSR. Anyone over 13 years old must HAVE a life jacket. If you are renting equipment, your outfitter will provide them.

title: Annual Dipsea Trail Detour Over Redwood Creek in Effect
description: The Dipsea Trail is closed between the Muir Woods parking lot and junction with the Deer Park Fire Road. Please detour via Muir Woods Road & the Deer Park Fire Road, which adds ~0.5 miles. This is a seasonal detour (roughly December ~ March) due to the footbridge over Redwood Creek being removed for spawning Coho salmon & Steelhead trout. It does not impact the main Redwood Creek Trail or access to Muir Woods National Monument.

title: Restrooms and Water Not Available at Visitor Center
description: Due to construction, the Visitor Center Restrooms and water fountains are currently unavailable. Temporary restroom facilities (not wheelchair accessible*) are located to the South East of the visitor center, by the flagpole near the parking lot. Please bring plenty of water for yourself and your group. *Wheelchair accessible restrooms are located 1/4 mi down the fully accessible Centennial trail at the entrance to the Lava Flow Campground.

title: Staff Unable to Check Phone Messages at the Visitor Center
description: At this time, staff are unable to check or receive phone messages left at the visitor center. All calls to the park are being routed to Tuzigoot National Monument. You can also reach the park at moca_tuzi_info@nps.gov if you need to contact the park.

title: Hazardous Icy Conditions
description: The visitor center at George Washington Birthplace National Monument is open, but travel through park grounds may be limited for visitor safety due to hazardous icy conditions.

title: Select Trails Require Day Use Pass from Woodstock Inn & Resort Nordic Center
description: Four miles of trail along the carriage road corridors have been designated as ‘Multi-Use Trails’ in winter – walkers are permitted to use these trails free of charge. All other trails require a day/season pass from the Woodstock Inn. See link for details.

title: Park Pass Not Needed
description: As Congaree does not have an entrance fee, we do not have staff dedicated to selling or issuing park passes. If interested in obtaining a park pass, you must call the day of your visit to inquire about staff availability. There is no guarantee you will be able to purchase or obtain a park pass at Congaree.

title: Plan Ahead for 2026 Construction
description: Parkwide construction is planned for 2026 and may affect travel in some areas. Colter Bay is anticipated to be a good option for visitors. See the Road Construction page for the latest updates.

title: Areas of Meridian Hill Park closed for repairs
description: The lower plaza and areas surrounding the cascading fountain of Meridian Hill Park are closed while the park undergoes repairs. Sidewalks along W and 16th Streets remain open to pedestrians. A pathway between W Street NW, the James Buchanan Memorial, the Dante Statue, and the upper terrace is open but will close at a later date for work on the statues. Much of the upper level of the park will close in spring and summer 2026 to rehabilitate the grass.

title: Dunes Drive Safety Corridor
description: Dunes Drive is open. A safety corridor is in effect between mile marker 3 and 5.5 along Dunes Drive. Visitors may drive but not stop or exit their vehicle in this area.

title: Pavement Project- Playalinda District
description: The Playalinda Beach District continues a Federal Highways pavement preservation project that started on Sept. 22nd, 2025. Visitors to the seashore could expect limited access to some parking areas and roadways to include the vistas.

title: Do Not Handle Volcanic Tephra
description: Park staff have cleared Crater Rim Drive East and Chain of Craters Road of tephra which includes volcanic ash, rock and glassy strands called Pele hair. The roads are now open following episode 41 of the Kīlauea eruption. Tephra is still present in many areas at the summit and can irritate your skin, eyes and respiratory system. For your safety, do not touch it.

title: East Portal Road Closed
description: The East Portal Road is closed for the winter season.

relatedRoadEvents.title: East Portal Road Closed

title: Hopeton Earthworks Closed for Construction Until May 2026
description: Hopeton Earthworks, located at 990 Hopetown Rd. Chillicothe, OH 45601, is closed for construction until May 2026. Parking lot improvements and construction activity will block safe access to cars and foot traffic at the trail heads. Call the park at 740-774-1126 for more information or visit the park's Mound City Group Visitor Center at 16062 State Route 104, Chillicothe, OH 45601.

title: Winter Weather Conditions Affecting Grounds Access and Safety
description: Park pathways may be inaccessible or treacherous due to winter weather conditions affecting the park, such as ice, snow, sleet, and heavy rains. Visitor should exercise caution when visiting park grounds.

title: Click the 'View all alerts' button below for additional park updates
description: Be sure to review the ALERT page before and during your visit for additional, up-to-date information on closures, cautions, and other current park conditions.

title: Temporary Phone Number for Visitor Center
description: Our main phone number for the visitor center is temporarily disconnected. Please call 307-877-4456 to reach a ranger.

title: 49 Palms Trail Weekday Closure
description: The 49 Palms Oasis trail is closed on weekdays so that park staff can repair rain damage. The trail is open on weekends.

title: Voicemail not functioning
description: The voicemail for the main park phone line (435-692-1234) is not functioning reliably. People are advised to call during business hours in order to speak with a ranger.

title: Voicemail not functioning
description: The voicemail for the main park phone line (970-562-4282) is not functioning reliably. People are advised to call during business hours in order to speak with a ranger.

title: Ohanapecosh Campground area closed for construction into spring of 2026.
description: The entire Ohanapecosh Campground area is CLOSED for the duration of the construction project, including all campsites, visitor center, restrooms, picnic area, & Ohanapecosh river access. The campground will open as soon after the completion of the project as possible.

title: Backcountry vehicle route closed for winter season
description: Vehicular access to Valles Caldera's backcountry is currently closed for the 2025-2026 winter season. During the off-season, the route will remain open to pedestrians, equestrians, cyclists, skiers, and snowshoers. The route is scheduled to reopen to vehicular traffic on May 15, 2026.

title: Flooding and Landslides Affecting Park Complex
description: The park complex experienced severe flooding and landslides from recent heavy rains, causing unsafe conditions and limiting basic infrastructure in some areas. Some services are available in Stehekin on winter operations.

title: Intermittent Closure of Park Loop Trailhead parking
description: From January 15 through March 5 there may be intermittent closures of the Park Loop Trail parking lot at Fayetteville Town Park as crews are working on a project to expand parking in that location.

title: Temporary Closures While World War II Memorial Updates Lighting, Fountains
description: The World War II Memorial is undergoing a modernization of its fountains and lighting to address aging infrastructure, improve reliability, and enhance the appearance of the memorial. Visitors may find temporary closures of parts of the memorial during their visit. For your safety and the safety of the contractors, please respect any area closures you encounter. We apologize for any inconvenience.

title: Temporary Closures While World War II Memorial Updates Lighting, Fountains
description: The World War II Memorial is undergoing a modernization of its fountains and lighting to address aging infrastructure, improve reliability, and enhance the appearance of the memorial. Visitors may find temporary closures of parts of the memorial during their visit. For your safety and the safety of the contractors, please respect any area closures you encounter. We apologize for any inconvenience.

title: Selma Interpretive Center Closed (Alternative Center Available)
description: The Selma Interpretive Center will remain closed to the public due to major renovations; project completion is expected by 2028. Rangers, parking, and other services are available at a temporary space, the 𝐒𝐞𝐥𝐦𝐚 𝐖𝐞𝐥𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐂𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫, located at 𝟏𝟒 𝐁𝐫𝐨𝐚𝐝 𝐒𝐭. There are two other unaffected visitor centers offering services: Montgomery Interpretive Center (1521 Harris Way, Montgomery, AL 36106) Lowndes Interpretive Center (7002 US-80, Hayneville, AL 36040)

title: We Do Not Mail Junior Ranger Books
description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

title: Smith Point Bridge Following Single Lane Traffic Pattern
description: Visitors driving to access the Otis Pike Wilderness and Smith Point County Park should be aware that the Smith Point Bridge is open currently following a single lane traffic pattern. There is a 15 ton vehicle weight limit.

title: Seasonal road closures
description: The following roads are closed for the season: Kuwohi Road, Roaring Fork Motor Nature Trail, Little Greenbrier Road, Heintooga Ridge Road, Forge Creek Road, Parson Branch Road, and Rich Mountain Road.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Seasonal road closures

title: Visitor Center Restrooms
description: The visitor center restrooms are currently being remodeled. Restrooms are available in a trailer just behind the restroom remodel.

title: Dredging at Mansfield Channel; Mile 55-60 of South Beach Closed
description: Starting January 18, 2026, access to the beach between Mile Markers 55 and 60 will be restricted as the US Army Corps of Engineers dredge the Mansfield Channel and place the dredged materials on land.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Dredging at Mansfield Channel; Mile 55-60 of South Beach Closed

title: Ongoing Road Projects and Helene Recovery
description: Parkway areas closed for ongoing road maintenance, construction, and Helene recovery projects.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Ongoing Road Projects and Helene Recovery

title: Pile Burning Winter 2026
description: The St. Croix National Scenic Riverway plans to burn numerous brush piles in the Riverway corridor from January through March of 2026. The National Park Service (NPS) is conducting these burns to improve prairie, savanna, and forest habitat along the St. Croix and Namekagon rivers.

title: Winter Road Closures
description: The Wheeler Peak Scenic Drive is closed for the season at Upper Lehman Creek Campground (mile 3). Snake Creek Road and Strawberry Creek Road are closed for the season at the park boundary. Baker Creek Road is closed at Grey Cliffs Campground. Click "more" for current road and campground closures.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Winter Road Closures

title: Tyler Bend Area Closures
description: Starting on January 20, 2026, the Tyler Bend Pavilion, Pavilion Parking Area, and Lower Launch river access will be closed to the public due to a construction project. The area is expected to open no later than March 31, 2026.

title: Former Golf Course Property
description: The former golf course property in Peninsula, including a section of the Valley Trail, is closed during environmental remediation.

title: Kennesaw Mountain Road Closed to Private Vehicles and Cyclists
description: Starting January 5th, 2026, the road to the top of Kennesaw Mountain will be closed to private vehicles and cyclists due to construction and painting. The park's Visitor Center is still accessible. This work is in preparation for the upcoming Safety Improvement Project. For updates, continue to check park's website.

title: Select restrooms
description: As of April 2, 2025, select restrooms are closed until further notice.

title: Visitor Center open 8:30am to 4:00pm, Wednesday to Sunday.
description: To complete an interior renovation construction project, the Visitor Center has been temporarily moved to the opposite end of the Visitor Center parking lot. The temporary Visitor Center is in a wheelchair accessible trailer. Please also note that the lake webcam is offline until construction is completed. Thank you for your patience.

title: A Few Small Roads Closed Due to Roadway Damage and/or Repair Work
description: These roads are closed due to roadway damage during past storm events and/or repairs to roadway damage: Middle Fork Rd, Redwood Canyon Rd, South Fork Rd, Crystal Cave Rd

relatedRoadEvents.title: Road Closures Related to Road Damage From and/or Repairs Of Past Storm Events

title: Generals Highway Closed For the Season Between Grant Grove and Giant Forest
description: Generals Highway is closed between Lodgepole Village and Montecito Lodge for the winter season. It is not possible to drive between Grant Grove/General Grant Tree in Kings Canyon National Park and Giant Forest and General Sherman Tree in Sequoia National Park. The closed section usually opens in late March, depending on snow conditions.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Generals Highway Seasonal Closure Between Sequoia and Kings Canyon National Parks

title: Winter Hazards on Trails - Downed Trees and High Water
description: While park staff clear trails several times each year, please be prepared for downed trees. Rain and wind continue to bring down dead trees within the Carr megafire burn scar, and this trend will continue for years. Also note high water at creek fords and seasonal streams during this time of year. Enjoy your national recreation area but please be safe out there.

title: Temporary Closure of Dunewood Campground
description: The Dunewood Campground will be closed May 3 through May 15, 2026, for repaving. Our alternate campsites still may have reservation spots available at the Dunbar Group Site, and the Central Avenue Walk-In Sites.

title: Visitor Center Alert
description: Visitor Center is open Friday through Monday 8:30am-4:00pm. Outdoor amenities (bathrooms, passport stamp station, drinking fountain) and grounds remain OPEN DAILY from sunrise to sunset.

title: Hospital wing, dining hall, shower room closed for rehabilitation work
description: Until approximately 2028, a portion of the cell house will be closed to visitors for important historic preservation work. We apologize for any inconvenience. Learn more at https://www.nps.gov/alca/alcatraz-historic-preservation-projects.htm

title: Bridge Closure on FM 2610
description: The bridge over Menard Creek on Farm to Market Road (FM) 2610 is closed for construction. Birdwatchers Trail is open and accessible from the south through Romayor.

title: Winter Seasonal Road Closure
description: The Scenic Drive past the visitor center is closed to vehicles for the winter season. When free of snow, the Scenic Drive is available for pedestrian use, including walking and biking. After it is snow-covered, skiing, snowshoeing, and walking are allowed. The visitor center is accessible and open 8:00 am-4:30 pm Monday through Saturday and closed seasonally on Sundays and federal holidays.

title: Expect Long Waits at the Summit Entrance Station Before Sunset
description: Due to a high volume of traffic, cars may be temporarily turned around at the gate when capacity is reached. Purchasing a pass online does not guarantee entry at sunset. To ensure timely access, arrive 3 hours before sunset.

title: Construction work at entrance to Visitor Center parking lot
description: Construction work at the entrance to the Visitor Center parking lot will cause single-lane road closures from January through March. Please use caution and follow signage or directions from workers.

title: Cottonwood and Horse Roundup Campground Closure June 28 - July 10
description: Cottonwood Campground and Horse Roundup Campground will close from June 28 - July 10, 2026, in support of a special event.

title: David Wills House Closure
description: The David Wills House is currently closed. No known reopening date is available. Please visit our David Wills House web page for more information and a 3D tour of the house.

title: Limited Parking and Traffic Delays in Summit District
description: Beginning Monday, January 12, 2026 Haleakalā Visitor Center (summit district) parking lot will have limited parking due to a water infrastructure project. Expect traffic delays. The project will last several months.

title: Mica View Trail work
description: The Mica View Trail in the east district of Saguaro National Park will be closed for resurfacing from January 5 through March 7, 2026. This accessible trail was last resurfaced in 2016, and the material has come to the end of its lifecycle. Access from the Broadway Trailhead and the Loop Drive is no longer available. Check in with Visitor Center staff for alternative routes.

title: Equestrian Traffic Temporarily Prohibited - Mile 124.5 to 155
description: Horses are prohibited between Little Tonoloway (mile 124.5) and Paw Paw (mile 155) due to recent and ongoing towpath resurfacing. See more for details.

title: No Boating on Lake Manly (Badwater Basin)
description: Launching or recovering any type of watercraft is prohibited. No flotation devices, including kayaks, paddleboards, or similar equipment, are permitted anywhere in the park.

title: Winter Season Closures
description: The Wall Street side of the Navajo Loop Trail is closed. The Bryce Connector Trail (between Bryce Point and Peekaboo Loop) is closed. The roads to Paria View and Fairyland Point are closed to vehicles.

title: Cholla Cactus Garden Trail Closed
description: The Cholla Cactus Garden trail is closed until late spring, so that park staff can improve trail conditions.

title: Lassen National Park Highway Closed for Winter
description: The main park road (Hwy 89) is closed at Kohm Yah-mah-nee Visitor Center (southwest entrance) and Loomis Plaza (northwest entrance) for the snow season. The road will not reopen until late spring or early summer. Please use caution if driving to the park and check weather forecasts.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Lassen National Park Highway Closed for Winter

title: Going-to-the-Sun Road Seasonal Closure
description: Going-to-the-Sun Road is now closed for the season at Lake McDonald Lodge on the west side of the park. On the east side, the closure is located 1.5 miles west of the St. Mary Visitor Center. Travel on open sections of the road may change at any time due to winter weather conditions. Visitors should check road conditions before their arrival and be prepared for winter weather.

title: Limited Access to Apollo District Parking Lots 2 and 3
description: Please be advised that parking areas #2 and #3 of Apollo Beach district (New Smyrna Beach) will be closed beginning January 5, 2026. These boardwalks are set for demolition and replacement. These areas will be closed to public access during construction. For your safety, please respect all posted closures and avoid entering closed areas of the seashore. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.

title: Difficult Conditions Complicate Cataract Canyon Takeouts
description: Hite Ramp is closed. The North Wash take-out is a primitive, unimproved dirt take-out. You will not be able to back a trailer down the dirt slope. Recent washouts have further deteriorated conditions.

title: Pet Policy
description: Pets are not allowed inside the visitor center at Aztec Ruins or on the trail through the Aztec West Great House. Pets are welcome in the parking lot, the picnic area, and along the Old Spanish Trail. Service animals are the only exception. Emotional support, therapy, and companion animals are not considered service animals and therefore not permitted in the visitor center or on the trail through Aztec West.

title: Museum to Remain Open During Construction
description: The Oklahoma City National Memorial Museum, Outdoor Symbolic Memorial, and Book Store will remain open during the construction of Foreward - a new Chapter of the museum. Visitors will enter through a new entrance on the southwest corner of the building. A temporary Visitor Experience Center has been placed near the museum for visitors to purchase tickets and browse Memorial merchandise. For more information on Foreward, please visit http://www.memorialmuseum.com.

title: Winter Seasonal Road Closures
description: The following roads are closed for the winter season or due to current snow conditions: Mineral King Rd, Highway 180 between Grant Grove and Cedar Grove, Main Sherman Tree Parking, Crescent Meadow and Moro Rock Roads, and Panoramic Point Road.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Winter Seasonal Closures

title: Water is Required
description: Due to the trail being steep of 12 percent grade, all visitors wishing to hike the Lower Cliff Dwelling Trail must bring a water bottle with them. No Exceptions.

title: The Use of Drones is Prohibited at Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area
description: Currently, the use of drones is prohibited at Big South Fork and all National Parks. Detailed information about the policy is available...

title: Potentially Deadly Poisoning Linked to the Consumption of Wild Mushrooms
description: An outbreak of serious illness associated with eating foraged wild mushrooms has been reported. CDPH is urging individuals to avoid foraging for and eating wild mushrooms while risk of amatoxin poisoning is high.

title: Park Headquarters Road closed
description: Park Headquarters Road is closed due to rehabilitation work on the Sugarlands water and wastewater systems.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Park Headquarters Road closed

title: Limited access to some trails due to washouts from winter storms.
description: The following trails have been impacted by winter storms: Carter Falls Trail footbridge over the Nisqually River has been dislodged and there is no safe water crossing; Kautz Creek Trail is impassable one mile from the trailhead due to a washout. There may be other trails with unreported storm impacts around the park.

title: Homestead Closure
description: The historic Andrew Johnson Homestead is closed. All tours and access to this structure are cancelled until further notice.

title: Roads to Butte Lake, Juniper Lake, and Warner Valley Closed for Winter
description: Roads to Butte Lake, Juniper Lake, and Warner Valley areas are closed to vehicle access due to snow. Roads leading to Juniper Lake and Warner Valley sustained significant damage outside of the park with no ETA for repair. *Note: snow machine use is prohibited within the boundaries of Lassen Volcanic National Park.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Roads to Butte Lake, Juniper Lake, and Warner Valley Closed for Winter

title: South Parking Lot of Greenbelt Park Closed to Vehicles until March 31, 2026
description: The south parking lot of Greenbelt Park will be closed to vehicle access until March 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. The area will remain available to pedestrians.

title: Greenbelt Park Perimeter Trail Closed until March 31, 2026
description: The section of the perimeter trail from the 0.8 mile marker to the 1.9 mile marker at Park Central Road is closed for repairs until March 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m.

title: Annual closures to better protect elephant seal pups will be in effect from Dec. 15 until March 31.
description: Annual beach closures for the elephant seal breeding season start December 15 and go through March 31. Closures include the southern end of South Beach, the beach from the Lifeboat Station to Chimney Rock, the Fish Dock area, and portions of Drakes Beach. Please stay out of closed areas for your safety and the safety of the seals.

title: Staple Bend Tunnel Closure
description: Due to a rockfall, the interior of the Staple Bend Tunnel is closed. The trail is still open. From the Mineral Point Trailhead, visitors can bypass the tunnel, via a path around the right side of the tunnel structure.

title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive closed for annual winter/spring season
description: The Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive is closed to vehicles for the annual winter and spring closure. The drive is open for hiking and walk-in recreation during the vehicle closure.

relatedRoadEvents.title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive annual winter/spring road closure

title: Ride Share Services - pickup limited
description: Taxi and ride-share services are often not available in Pecos. Even if services like Uber and Lyft will bring you here, it can be very difficult to secure a ride out. Please ensure you have a departure plan in place before being dropped off at Pecos.

title: Accessibility Lifts at Fort Sumter Inoperable Until Further Notice
description: The accessibility lifts at Fort Sumter are currently out of order. The only way to access level 2 and level 3 of the Fort is via a staircase until further notice.

title: Temporary closures possible if parking is unavailable
description: If parking lots are full, there is a temporary closure of the park entrance at Georgetown Pike & Old Dominion Dr. Park re-opens when space is available. No waiting, no street parking. Updates at Great Falls Park Facebook page.

title: Fort Pickens Campground Restroom E Loop Closure
description: The park is remodeling the interior of the restroom structure at loop E. All other restrooms will remain open for campers with reservations during this time.

title: Temporary Pause on Research and Donation Requests
description: Please be advised that due to staffing changes and the ongoing hiring freeze, there is a pause on research requests and donation considerations at all parks in the Manhattan Sites Unit until further notice. Please check this website and social media for updates.

title: Temporary Pause on Research and Donation Requests
